sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: Apple의 2025 회계연도 매출은 4,161억 6,100만 달러로 전년 대비 6 % 증가하며 견조한 성장세를 보였습니다.

keyPoints.text: Services 부문 매출은 109,158백만 달러로 전년 대비 14 % 성장하며 핵심 동인으로 작용했습니다.

riskChanges.text: 2025년 2분기부터 중국, 인도, 한국 등을 포함한 국가들에 대한 새로운 미국 관세가 발표되어 공급망 및 비용 구조에 불확실성이 증대되었습니다.
글로벌 거시경제 상황, 인플레이션, 환율 변동 및 반도체 관련 무역 규제 조사가 사업 성과에 잠재적인 부정적 영향을 미칠 수 있습니다.

businessChanges.text: iPhone 매출은 Pro 모델의 판매 호조에 힘입어 209,586백만 달러를 기록하며 전년 대비 4 % 증가했습니다.
지역별로 일본(+15 %), 유럽(+10 %), 미주(+7 %) 지역에서 매출 성장이 두드러졌으나, 중화권(Greater China)은 64,377백만 달러로 전년 대비 (4) % 감소했습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 2025 회계연도 영업이익은 1,330억 5,000만 달러, 순이익은 1,120억 1,000만 달러를 기록했습니다.
총자산은 3,592억 4,100만 달러이며, 현금및현금성자산은 359억 3,400만 달러를 보유하고 있습니다.

watchItems.text: Services 부문의 높은 마진(75.4 %)이 전체 수익성을 견인하고 있으나, 지정학적 관세 리스크가 하드웨어 제품군(Products)의 마진 압박 요인으로 작용할지 모니터링이 필요합니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: FY2025 revenue = 4,161억 6,100만 달러 (USD 416.161B; 416,161 million), period ended 2025-09-27.
FY2025 operating income = 1,330억 5,000만 달러 (USD 133.05B; 133,050 million), period ended 2025-09-27.
FY2025 net income = 1,120억 1,000만 달러 (USD 112.01B; 112,010 million), period ended 2025-09-27.
FY2025 total assets = 3,592억 4,100만 달러 (USD 359.241B; 359,241 million), period ended 2025-09-27.
FY2025 cash and cash equivalents = 359억 3,400만 달러 (USD 35.934B; 35,934 million), period ended 2025-09-27.

Macroeconomic and Industry Risks The Company’s operations and performance depend significantly on global and regional economic conditions and adverse economic conditions can materially adversely affect the Company’s business, results of operations, financial condition and stock price. The Company has international operations with sales outside the U.S. representing a majority of the Company’s total net sales. In addition, the Company’s global supply chain is large and complex and a majority of the Company’s supplier facilities, including manufacturing and assembly sites, are located outside the U.S. As a result, the Company’s operations and performance depend significantly on global and regional economic conditions. Adverse macroeconomic conditions, including slow growth or recession, high unemployment, inflation, tighter credit, higher interest rates, and currency fluctuations, can adve

Macroeconomic Conditions Macroeconomic conditions, including inflation, interest rates and currency fluctuations, have directly and indirectly impacted, and could in the future materially impact, the Company’

Tariffs and Other Measures Beginning in the second quarter of 2025, new U.S. Tariffs were announced, including additional tariffs on imports from China, India, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, Vietnam and the EU, among others. In response, several countries have imposed, or threatened to impose, reciprocal tariffs on imports from the U.S. and other retaliatory measures. Various modifications to the U.S. Tariffs have been announced and further changes could be made in the future, which may include additional sector-based tariffs or other measures. For example, the U.S. Department of Commerce has initiated an investigation under Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962, as amended, into, among other things, imports of semiconductors, semiconductor manufacturing equipment, and their derivative products, including downstream products that contain semiconductors.
